text: We present a self-propelled colloidal “docker” made of hematite that can be controlled with precision to pick up, carry and spew out large particle sized loads. Upon exposure to visible light, the docker can bind, transport and release the load at desired locations. The propulsion, picking up and spewing out of the cargo particles is fully reversible and photo triggered. The navigation and movement can be carried out either in a weak uniform magnetic field or along the nanotracks fabricated within the texture of the substrate. The mechanism of both propulsion and cargo docking and releasing is based on an osmotic/phoretic driven flow mediated by fuel (hydrogen peroxide) gradients generated photo catalytically at the hematite surface. Unlike state of the art multi component bio-hybrid robots these single component iron oxide colloidal particles can be fabricated in large quantities. Hence this system presents itself with enormous potential in design of large micrometer sized artificial factories and for the realisation of bio-mimicked functional complex systems.
